reviews.review_text: Great location and well maintained property.  I’ll visit again.

Always a blast with our Caravan, get a beach side plot
Update 14/5/2026 - arrived again for a quick long weekend, bathrooms were clean front service was good, camp site clean, ready for us to just have a blast
With the exception of one jammed blind (I fixed) and poor tv reception (Gave up using) nice clean room, very comfortable and included all that we needed.
Very clean amenities, lovely staff, good location, and nice Thai restaurants close by.

My wife and I had booked a disabled cabin (12 months in advance) as my wife is disabled-for weekend of1st 3rd May. My wife received a phone call from park saying we couldn’t use the room due to plumbing and electrical issues and could not be fixed in time. We travel with friends who have caravans and campers. We were told that we need to find alternate accommodation. Which is very difficult for disabilities.
We had dinner with our travelling companions at their caravan, I decided to walk past the cabin we were supposed to stay in and found someone sitting in cabin watching TV. I then went and approached the managers and asked why someone else was in our precooked cabin. The reason given was unacceptable and obviously another lie. I was told they had to take them in as where they were booked was closed down(lie number3).
The other occupants should have been made to find other accommodation, not us.
The following night when the people were still there, we noticed that their vehicle, parked in disabled parking, was not displaying disabled parking permit and that those staying were very able young people,
Our companions, some who have been going therefore over 20 years were disgusted with the way we were treated and will not return.
The attitude of the manager was basically she didn’t care, and it wasn’t her problem.
All they offered was 2 free nights , but not in holidays or busy times.
The distress caused to my wife was terrible, and to have the manager un sympathetic and having continued lie to us was unprofessional and unacceptable .
I give 1star because I can’t go lower

Gavan Hall
Awesome spot. Total relaxation!!!
Beautiful location,lovely friendly staff,look forward to returning.
Such a relaxing place. Close to beaches & shops
free wifi and also beach view

Great catavan site backing right on the water. Easy check in ( all details needed sent by email!) Such a great area for a few relaxing days . Not overly expensive for a clean  well run and kept caravan park. Cheers

Well located busy holiday park on the waterfront, close to the main street and nearby Shell Cove marina precinct. Staff kindly fitted us in to a powered site at short notice. Amenities were ok given the amount of people staying... the grey colour scheme is serviceable but a bit grim. We appreciated and made use of the herb garden near the barbecue area & camp kitchen.

Nrma shell harbour is a well situated park with good common facilities which were clean and well kept.

The lower price of the park reflects good value for what is provided. The van sites were reasonable size but quite difficult to get into,.grassed or some concrete.

I would recommend this place to travellers as it was only a 500m walk to town and cheaper

For absolute beach front stunning sites, probably the best we've stayed at. Spectacular views and couldn't get any closer to the beach and rock pools.
Some small criticisms, nothing major, just little things.
The park is very busy, always.  Understandable, but if a lot of other vans isn't your thing, take note.
The kerbs on the beach front sites are huge, so, if you use any sort of mobility aid, be warned.
A defined path through the middle sites to the beach and amenities would be a good idea, otherwise you are either walking through someone's site or it's a bit of a walk via the road, not a problem for an able bodied walker, it's not that far, but once again, for someone using a mobility aid,  the navigation can be tricky.
Amenities were good, very well appointed and clean, but doors into the disabled facilities are very tight for a wheelchair. Internal facilities in the disabled bathroom spacious and reasonably easy to navigate.
I could only see a LH disabled facility, made it a little more difficult for a RH side affected person  but nothing insurmountable.
Just little observations, honestly no complaint, but might help other more disabled people to navigate.
Overall a great location,  shops,  cafes,  restaurants right on the doorstep, or just a short drive to a major shopping centre or the marina development.
Will definitely be back!

One of our favourite campsites. Great pool and loads of facilities just a short walk away.
Back again next week!!
Lovely place to stay on weekends with dog - really appreciate this, thank you for your service 😊

Caravan Park
Clos3 to ocean pool and great beach
View from van is especially good

We stayed at the NRMA Park, Shellharbour. The park has undergone some renovations since we were here last.  Continues to improve.  Odd, all booking made via phone and online, so no face to face interaction with staff.  Entry code provided, so drive straight in and find your assigned site.||Once settled in he park is a short walk to local pub Ocean Beach Hotel, Ocean pool (note closed for cleaning, every Tuesday afternoon) and local cafes and shops. Plus 1.5km walk to Shell Cove Marina . ||Dog friendly everywhere, except the beach out the front,  which is disappointing but other than that,  gr8 place to visit. ||Local told us to join the Feros App, to get half price drinks and a two for one meal deal at The Ocean Hotel.  I had the Poke bowl with salmon was amazing.  This pub has undergone renos, gr8 improvement. Needs dog water bowls in the designated dog area.
Need to book well in advance

Great view and location.
The amenities were clean and in mostly good condition.
The sites are a great size, but can still be difficult to get into.

However, the last two days we were there the bins were overflowing. We had to close up our camper due to the overwhelming smell.
It was not a pleasant end to our holiday.

Rigtig fint sted, med venligt personale, generelt rene faciliteter som bad og toilet.
Vi fik en god campsite plads, 2 række til vandet.
Ikke så meget andet spændende på pladsen, byen og stranden var også relativt stille.
1 nat for 47$, fin pris.

My favourite beach.
It has life savers to keep us safe and a lovely walk to Shellcove.
Beautiful  park amazing  staff Ryan and Julia are very accomadating friendly people who go out of their way to ensure the park is always presentable friendly ,clean will definitely be back
Point cabin on top of the headland rocks...sound of waves on rock. Walk 5m to beach. Fragrance of salt.

I was stopping by for one night and booked the first Oceanside villa with  bunk beds,but the top bunk was dirty and had rubbish on the floor and had a smell. There were also a lot of insects and we felt something in the blanket. Other than that, there were no bedsheets on any of the bunk beds. We had booked the cabin for 6 pax but we only received two towels.

Stayed in a park cabin. Super clean and well maintained.  Had a small issue with a sliding door which was dealt with promptly by park management. This cabin had inclusions i wasn't expecting,  such as iron,  hair-dryer, toiletries, and extra blankets.  As a solo traveller on this occasion I felt super safe, and with no vehicle , the number of facilities available within walking distance was excellent.  Highly recommend.
Great camping spot. Friendly staff, great amenities!
Nice place to camp
Great place highly recommend it 👍

We stayed at Shellharbour NRMA for a few nights in our caravan. The location itself is great and we really enjoyed having beach access, but overall the park didn’t quite meet our expectations.

When we arrived it was fairly quiet, but it became very busy over the weekend. The park is currently undergoing renovations and adding new sites, though some of the layouts looked quite tight with poles and close proximity to other vans — I’m not sure how easy it will be for larger setups to manoeuvre once it’s finished.

There wasn’t a lot for kids within the park, which wasn’t ideal, but it did encourage us to explore the surrounding area, which turned out to be a highlight. We loved being close to the beach, but our site became a bit of a thoroughfare for kids walking directly through to the sand. A designated walkway or clearer access point would really help improve privacy for those staying on the beachfront.

The main downside for us was the amenities. Unfortunately, the bathrooms didn’t appear to be cleaned during our stay, leaving toilet paper, sand, and water scattered around. This was disappointing compared to other parks we’ve stayed at.

Overall, the location is beautiful, and with some improvements to the amenities and general upkeep, the park could become a much better experience for guests. We enjoyed the beach access, but we’d hope to see a few upgrades before considering a future stay.

Nice location cabins very substandard .verandah  dirty flooring. Fly screens ripped.Fly screen windows disguisting dirty .Out side table as out last it's use by date .Bedrooms okay .Toilet  door dooes not lock
Bathroom  very nice .For $330 a night.Expect a little better maintained for sure .
Cabin we stayed in was a little run down. Looks like it has had some water damage in the bathroom.|Ideal spot right on the water, other amenities were clean and tidy.

Where do I start?   |The online photo of the Beachside Cabin view of sea was totally different to what we found - apart from a glimpse, virtually none.  The filthy windows and split insect screen started to dent the expectations.  The cockroach running across the kitchen floor was beginning to worry us too, as it had been feeding on the food crumbs left lying around by the cleaner, as had the ants!|Anyway, we moved in and began washing utensils etc and sprayed as much as we could.  Bathroom was great, beds looked good but WiFi wasn’t working.  Checked with the office to be told that WiFi doesn’t work in the cabins - but we’d been supplied with the code?…duh!|Went to use the beach ‘access’ path, only to find it was grossly overgrown and had a nearby warning about snakes!|Got thru the night and had a look around Shellharbour the next morning.|Got back in time for lunch and put the AC on in preparation for the high 30’s temps for later in the day.  Freezing cold in the lounge but hot in the bedrooms!|Would have been nice to sleep with some fresh breeze but being unable to lock the screen door interfered with that idea.|Generally, the park looks good but some of the cabins may have been seriously neglected.  The guy in the office thanked me for the feedback and said he’d pass it on!  Mate, I’d think they’d probably know already.|You can’t ask that sort of money for very poorly presented and misrepresented accommodation, very disappointing.  Sorry.

Lovely location. Poor security. I’ve had things stolen several times. Also been locked out of services several times, seems their computer system is a little questionable. And when you do question it, you might be accused of lying 😳😳

We stayed in a beachside cabin - having stayed in cabins at lots of caravan parks for many years and many times - this would have to be worst. Lounge was stained, kitchen drawer falling off, patches on wall - just old, tired and in need of updating. I hated every moment. Single beds are not made - you have to do this yourself. It is Council leased park so it is run on a shoe string budget. If I could give 0 stars I would

We had a powered site overlooking the bay. We saw whales playing and even baby whales plus to top it off dolphins 🐬 you couldn't ask for much more. The park itself is very clean and the staff were very helpful with directions to shops which are a few minutes walk north or the shopping complex twenty minute walk to the south, as well as tourist drives and attractions. A must place to stay on the south Coast.

3rd time visiting this caravan park and NEVER have been disappointed
Always lucky to get Foreshore Site and this time spent a few hours  watching a few Whales having a great time in the water from  our site   Incredible
Amenities spotless and all.staff so very friendly and helpful
Great stay ❤️❤️❤️
I understand this place is busy for a long weekend but to be screamed at over the phone for asking about availability is a joke . Maybe control your tone of voice Bret
Love the location and accommodation. Great place to stay we keep coming back
Beautiful small park with Excellent amenities and views. Great park for kids.

We stopped here in Aug '25 for an overnight stop over on a road trip north.  We stayed in a powered site (29).  The sites were a nice size with views of the beach.  The park was close enough to town for a meal (but we wouldn't go to the pub there again).  The amenities were very clean and not to far from our site.  It was perfect for an overnighter.

We have just spent a week here on our travels down south. I can say this is the best caravan park we have stayed in. Amazing location only 20m from the beaches. The staff are so friendly and the place is immaculate! Toilets and showers so clean . Camp kitchen is also amazing !
Highly recommend this park 😊
Natalie Butler
We love coming here for weekends away in our caravan.  Right on the water with unrestricted views!!!   Beautiful, peaceful and only 1.5 hours from home.
Beautiful and quiet place right at the beach. Great amenities and very clean. The kids loved it.

The staff were all friendly and helpful. The Caravan park is in a beautiful location right on the sea front and the rock pool is a few metres away from the Park. Beautiful view and sound of waves.
It is a different set up to other Caravan parks. The Amenities, BBQ, playground and kitchen is set in the middle of the Park and cabins are set around it and then the Caravans ( it just seems a strange arrangement)
Colourful lights show the area at night, well lit at night. The Amenities were very clean, every sink had hand soap. The hand dryer in the ladies Amenities is broken ( the council has been notified by the staff, the council is responsible for getting it fixed or replaced). I mentioned to the maintenance man regarding the broken shower heads in the males showers and was told that the customers keep damaging them, unsure if it is lack off maintenance or if nearly all shower heads were damaged by Campers.
One big problem was the lack of hot water in the showers, ( especially men's showers) the water wasn't hot for the duration of the shower, it turned cold and lukewarm and was especially on cold evenings unpleasant. This was confirmed by other people I spoke to.
Apart from the mentioned maintenance issues,
we did like the Caravan park and enjoyed our stay.

Can’t fault the location of the park, you are on the doorstep of the beach and a few minutes walk to the town centre. We are travelling in a caravan and have a 1.5 year old and it’s great to have a park, big bbq facilities and camp kitchen altogether and close by. Unfortunately the family bathroom is probably one of the worst we’ve come across in a big 4 park. Basically unusable for families with babies, hot water on all occasions was not working, and nowhere to lay your child down to dry them and get them dressed. The room itself looks like it’s meant for more of a laundry type set up. We ended up showering our daughter in the main facilities where there was hot water and having to run her back to our caravan to get dry and dressed. Womens toilet overall was clean, hand dryer not working the 2 weeks we were there.
Love the vibe

Great location great staff willing to go out of their way to help, being an NRMA member our savings were beyond our expectations. The NRMA Shellharbour Beachside holiday park is nice, quiet and peaceful with only a 2 minute walk to the beach, I would not hesitate to recommend the NRMA Shellharbour Beachside holiday park to any of my family and friends.
Great spot on the beach walking distance to shops pub etc

All the facilities are clean and well maintained. Great camp kitchen, with 4 bbq areas. They even have a Wood Fire pizza oven.|Great little mini supermarket also.|Place is great for families, couples and right on the water.|They have a variety of sites and we had a powered site, but There are cabins and all accommodation variety available.

WE STAYED IN 3 Different cabins & not 1 was ok )    :  BLACK MOLD ON CEILING FAN & IN AIR CON .THE CABIN SHOWERS LEAK . THE SHOWER  DRAIN HOLE OVER FLOWS. NO WAY TO BLOCK TOO MUCH LIGHT AT NIGHT . NO HAIR DRYER.
THE MANAGMENT IS SMUG .

This is a gem of a park. The facilities are spotless and thoroughly cleaned daily. The location is amazing, right next to the ocean baths and a very short walk to local cafes, restaurants and an IGA. No need to drive anywhere for your entire stay. Great value, super helpful staff and a safe, friendly atmosphere.
Awesome location friendly staff
Nice park that has beach front spots when available.
We took The Point and view was magic , reception were awesome no trouble too big and very clean

Good spot, the actual slabs and caravan spots are shabby but ok.
The actual park itself is looking a bit rundown.
They are getting rid of old permanent vans ATM that will help.
The amenities however after excellent.
Close to shops beach and pool. Lovely views.
The new Shell Cove marina precinct is a nice 1km walk down the beach or a 2 minute drive.
Great for a bike ride and fishing.

The accommodation seems a little dated and run down. Some of the items were very old and not well maintained. There was no WiFi connectivity as well. A little disappointed with the cottage house given that it didn’t come cheap as well.

Another annual return to this park this time in a motor home spent 4 nights on a beach front site then 2 nights smaller site after being moved along with other guests and there vans most disappointing thing was watching the owners of permanent vans having to rip down there dreams and holiday homes for obviously some greedy intent there are many permanent sites that are all deadline for destruction 😢

Staff very kindZ

All places were way cleaner than i expected.

Recommend!!!
Very good place

Toilet cleaning should be done in the afternoon when most people is out.Every time we need to use it in the morning the things got the yellow chain preventing people from using it. There was a back up toilet next to the Laundry but is a single one. No nearly enough for all the people in the place 😡
Had a great weekend so close to the water, ocean pool and shops.
Will go back!

Small tight park, tricky in a large van.
Good clean amenities and online check-in
Paid $71 night, over priced for this park
The cabin was in need of a refresh. The service was pleasant enough. The location was marvellous as we could walk to dinner close by. We could see the sea between all the other trailers & caravans.

We stayed at NRMA Shellharbour Holiday Park for three days and had a great experience. Our room was clean and well-stocked with essentials, including towels, shampoo, cutlery, plates, glasses, a full-sized fridge, and a microwave. The amenities were excellent—clean and well-equipped with cooking tools. The park also features a spacious common toilet and shower area, as well as a good-sized laundry facility.

Worth noting was the direct access to the rock pool and the Shellharbour Beach that leads to the breakwater.  Watching the sunrise each morning was a beautiful experience. The atmosphere was relaxing, and the people we met during our stay were lovely. Would definitely recommend!

So disappointed to hear this news. Another sign of companies bullying everyday Australians. Pushing out long term residents to help council and developers make big money $$$.

Phil has been holidaying at the NRMA Shellharbour Beachside Holiday Park south of Sydney for 23 years.

Recently he spent upwards of $50,000 renovating his caravan to better enjoy the picturesque coastal escape.

But he and several other caravan park regulars are soon to be booted from the site, after Shellharbour City Council announced they are going to convert annual sites into casual rentals

Don't support them.
Lovely beach

What would have been a great stay was ruined when we had a bike stolen (one of at least 2 that night). The manager did not seem at all surprised to hear our lock had been cut & was well aware that the “criminals” knew exactly where the park cameras are & how to avoid them. Surely if this park is rife with criminal activity there needs to be more duty of care to warn your patrons that extra precautions need to be taken!
Sensational caravan park.
Love it

Booked a powered camp site.
Perfect location. Short walk to beach and village. Not far from the new Marina. Will be back.

Caravan park right on the water.  It was so nice and cool and comfortable in the hot summer to get a sea breeze.
We had an unpowered site in a small van, so we loved it, but i would say if you are in a large caravan it can be a tight squeeze. Everyone is just a little bit too close to each other.
The shower/toilet block was large and the best I've been in. So clean and well kept.

Stellplätze direkt am Strand
Super schöner Camping ground. klein und süß.es is alles da
Waschmaschine Trockner Wäscheleinen super liebes Personal, Pool in die Ecke das Meer direkt da.
Awesome place .great service .Julia helped us out .so far in our road trip shellharbour nrma is best ever. Thanks heaps .Tee n Ling we will stay there again .
Campsites along the beach, very friendly staff and clean facilities. Spots are small, but this is standard near the beach
Location is 5 out of 5 but the rooms are way too small, the bunk bed is way too high especially for kids and my bedsheets and blankets were dirty.

I cannot recommend NRMA Shellharbour Beachside Holidays highly enough. The level of service and understanding Julia and Ryan gave after we experienced some car difficulties was next level. We will definitely come back!

Absolutely beauty. Very friendly and super clean. Had everything needed for a comfortable stay. Walking distance to Pub and Resteraunts.  Beautiful Ocean pool and Beaches. Will definitely recommend  this place for camping or caravan  stay |
Very clean, close to the beach/pool and shops. Fantastic place to stay, friendly & helpful staff. Can't wait to go back.

Nice van park,  right on the water.
Clean and tidy.
4pm Saturday live music 🎶 til 6pm.

I enjoyed a powered caravan site in this lovely park.
Great location
Friendly staff
Immaculate amenities
Views to die for

We were on site 24, and it was extremely hard to get on site. The Uphill site made positioning Van very difficult.  We have a 17-foot van. God help you if you're bigger. To make getting on site worse, there was a low fence on the other side, so reversing in is very hard.
Amenities were very good, but sites need a major upgrade.
Chris Davies.

Great Caravan Park right on the waterfront.
Cafes & Shops are a 5 min walk.
Very friendly and helpful staff/owners.
Very clean amenities, laundry & camp kitchen. Pet friendly.

Lovely location. I visited a friend while they were staying here at a powered site and absolutely beautiful location. Right on the beach and very close to the main street with eateries, salt water pools, park etc

Stayed here for work, booking in was easy, the park is close to the main street and all the restaurants, it's in a great position and surrounded by the sea, you can hear the waves from the cabin.|Cabin was good, but for what you get, i think it's on the dearer side

What's the point of being dog friendly and only allowing one dog on a site my dad and I are travling together and have a dog each and are not going to pay for 2 sites just because of the 2 dogs rediculas......

We stayed for 2 nights, and the views were pretty spectacular as we were right on the beach. The amenities are second to none. it looks like they have been recently built. The powered sites were a bit tight but understandable as this is an older caravan park before caravans became so large. We only have a small van, so it's fine for us.
Nice setting by the sea. Good facilities & service.
We stayed in the Caravan park & every thing was very good.
This is my favourite beach and has been since I was a kid.

We stayed in a tent. The size for the tent is really small and we were all squashed in.
The location is great but they advertise dog friendly and you literally can't even take the dog onto the beaches that you have access to. So If you want to enjoy the beach, someone needs to stay with dig which was pretty hard when you have kids.
Ok for one night stopover
Nice park great location. Stayed here a few times but we now have 2 dogs so can’t go back and stay there with them - only allow 1 dog per site. Pity cause we like the park and close to home.
Great location on the south end of the beach on John Street . Shops , pub & walking tracks are close by .
View from our caravan Beautiful spot walk into village 😍

We had a waterfront site for our caravan, which was amazing. Shops and restaurants are a 5 minute walk, and so are the ocean baths .
Mandy was very helpful and friendly she is amazing! We will definitely be back.
Very clean . Very good services.  BUT IT IS NOT BEACHFRONT . I've booked a beach cabin.  ⛱️

What a great location, but don’t tell anyone so I can rebook a powered caravan site, right on the beach, a great selection of local restaurant and a wonderful clean caravan park and staff with big smiles.

Used a cabin and was ok. Front door needs replacing on cabin 2, location is close to beach, salt 50mtr pool and restaurants. If you need WiFi, bring your own. 500mb a day and very slow. Cabin cost was too pricey in my opinion.

Great little caravan park right on the water.  Pretty busy during school holidays and quite small van sites. Amenities are really well looked after, and the customer service is great. Huge bonus - the site a pet friendly and welcome our fur kids.

Great facilities, with lots of bbqs, recycling, ice, swap a bottle, ice creams, laundry, common areas, live entertainment, pizza oven, dog friendly, water front views and a super friendly team to look after you. I highly recommend a stay here if you are in to area.
Bloody beauty of a caravan Park.Walking distance to shops and don't forget the pub.Swimming beach and ocean pool out the front of park
The wind is crazy but we had a great time 😎

This place is right on the coast in shell harbour 600m away from all the shops. The pool is right next to the caravan park and so is the swimming beach. A walk round the harbour and there is an awesome play ground. The facilities here are clean and tidy. Also very modern. The kitchen is great and plenty of hot plates for bbq. Kitchen is stocked with everything. Great little playground to keep the kids busy whilst cooking. The van sites are right on the water and are great. Prices are good with the NRMA discount.

Great location, clean bathroom and amenities, plus a pet friendly campsite!
Went during a busy weekend and the staff was super accommodating, got my tent right next to the beach entrance and my pup definitely loved the campground!

Stayed in a beach hut from 21/4 to 29/4. Awesome place, helpful friendly staff, beautiful views,very clean accommodation and amenities. Take a 5 min walk and you'll find a huge play park complete with zip line,ocean beach hotel, clothes shops, IGA,cafes and restaurants
Thankyou Shellharbour beachside holiday park for an excellent breakaway from home. We had great fun on the zipline(all of us are adults)
Great views

Great location on the beach and near rock pools.  Awesome public ocean pool with a kids pool as well. Stroll to decent eateries.  Did some kayaking on Lake Illawarra and encountered a sea turtle and caught plenty of fish.  Developments at nearby Shell Cove great. Amenities clean and relatively new.  Loved my stay here.

Great park in a beautiful location, friendly staff
We had a great time, and we will come back for sure

Beware a scam. Road service users get ripped off here. There are promised discounts, but they are phoney, and you get charged a higher amount than what the advertising said. Desk staff say the money is theirs now, and you won't get it back. Avoid this place at all costs- it's not worth the stress.
Nice park will stay  again
Great park beautiful location and facilities
What a great place to stay for a week in a cabin by the beach short walks to the pub shops and beaches very nice
Stayed here for two nights recently in a cabin and Was very clean and tidy.

We used to love staying here and this is like the 4th consecutive year but, this is the 1st time we brought our samoyed pup. Turns out that they are pet friendly as long as your dog doesn’t bark at all. Reception approached us twice (while having lunch and waiting in the toilet) and told us to control my playful’s dog barking. He wasn’t barking all the time, only when people greets him. Won’t recommend it to dog’s owner. Very upset. Won’t be coming back again.
Great spot on the coast, cant get much closer to the surf, we had a van site, huge amenities us plenty for the kids to do, close to town

We stayed at Shellharbour for 2 nights. It was lovely. The park location was amazing and the view from our water site was superb ❤️
I wish we had had more time there.
We loved our stay here. Great cabins, phenomenal location and clean facilities
Cabins are very clean and love the cabin.
Great place to stay with caravan

Came back from 3 nights with a group of four families from this Shellharbour Campground.

A lady next to our site already made the first 10 minutes of our stay, uncomfortable.
My wife and kids, walked through her site, and she raised her arms up in the air.  I've then reminded my wife and kids, friends as well regarding this incident and we all avoided walking through her camp site.

However she walked through, right in front of two of my friends tents countless times throughout our stay.  A typical hypocrite.

A few hours later, she came over and complained about our dogs barking.  After I told her that the dogs will eventually cease, she became agitated and further tried to compare dogs to babies.

My mate received a threatening email the morning after, stating that complaints were made by people, and if our dogs were not controlled, they will make us leave.

To be fair, this campground is not dog friendly. The beach does not allow dogs which should be stated on the website.

We will not be back here ever again, and for this place to carry the name "NRMA"  is staining the reputation of a great company.

The toilets were atrocious.  Faeces were stained on the metal handlebar in the far crucible for at least a solid 2 nights.  The crucibles were scattered with toilet paper and rolls on the floor, faeces stained on the wall of the toilets and the sink basins were filthy with stains of toothpaste and whatnot which was never wiped down.

Sunday morning was the first time the toilet was cleaned by an elderly gentleman. Other than that, the state of the toilet was appalling.

Staff in the office was a mixed bag.  One lady was very kind and wanted to help, but the manager/supervisor questioned her kindness and did not allow her to further assist us due to "policy" procedures.  Service from this campground is not up to par compared to other NRMA campgrounds that we have been to.

Toller Campingplatz, uns hat es sehr gut gefallen. Die Lage direkt am Wasser ist wirklich top. Die Sanitäranlagen waren sauber und ordentlich und auch groß genug. Direkt nebenan ist das Meerwasserschwibecken und der Strand ist auch ganz nah. Zum Ort sind es auch nur ca. 500m. Das Personal ist super freundlich und konnte uns weiterhelfen, wir wollten den Aufenthalt verlängern, leider war der Platz bereits ausgebucht, das Personal hat uns daher andere Plätze in der Region empfohlen - sehr nett.

We stayed at NRMA Shellharbour for just one night in one of their cabins, which was incredibly convient especially because many caravan parks won’t allow one night stays. We really enjoyed our short stay! The staff in the park were incredibly passionate, friendly and understanding! The cabin was spacious, with modern furnishings, it was just that the cabin itself felt old, such as the shower and the sliding doors easily came off the rails and the pots smelt a little and we couldn’t use them. However, the location was perfect by the beach and very very close to a shopping centre and petrol! The park also seemed incredibly cramped which felt slightly claustrophobic and loud at times. But still would highly recommend if you are staying in the area or need something close to Wollongong
We are currently staying here in our Caravan. Beautiful park in front of the beach. It's very clean, staff are friendly and helpful
Staff was wonderful, Mick, Kim and Mandy
Great location, fantastic camp kitchen
Great for an ocean dip in the morning

Right on the water this cvan park has good views and great walking distance into the main street of shell harbour. Amenities clean and close, lots of grass on sites and a general good feel to the park.
A lovely location, close to Shellharbour Village.
They are not happy if you check out late.

Our esky got stolen the second night we were there. The last time I stayed here a few years back my car/wallet/sunglasses/surfboard/beers got stolen. Seems to be a security issue in this area as I have never experienced such thievery whilst camping. Kind of disappointing as it would be nice to be able to go on a holiday without having to worry about my things getting stolen. Probably wouldn't recommend, maybe stay somewhere else if you don't want things to get stolen
Wonderful Park On the Beach
Lovely spot close to town, with water views.
What a Great little  place. In one of the cabins that overlooks the ocean. Check in was easy and fast
